title: rosa rubiginosa var. echinocarpa
intro: Rosa rubiginosa var. echinocarpa, also known as the wild rose or fragrant rose, is a medicinal plant species native to Europe and Asia. It is prized for its fragrant flowers, which have been used in traditional medicine for centuries to treat various health issues. The plant's medicinal properties include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it beneficial for wound healing, reducing pain and inflammation, and preventing infections. Rosa rubiginosa var. echinocarpa can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in topical applications to promote skin health and reduce symptoms of eczema and acne.
